metadata.title: Poesía: 登鹳雀楼 (On the Stork Tower)
metadata.description: A classic Tang Dynasty poem by Wang Zhihuan about gaining a higher perspective.

sentences.original: 白日依山尽，黄河入海流。
sentences.translation: The white sun sets behind the mountains; The Yellow River flows into the sea.

sentences.original: 欲穷千里目，更上一层楼。
sentences.translation: To see a thousand miles further, Ascend another story of the tower.

chunks.meaning: Desiring to exhaust / see to the end
one more level of the building.

sentences.original: 登高望远。 (Dēng gāo wàng yuǎn.)

cards.translation: to lean against / to depend on

sentences.translation: The setting sun leans against the mountain.

sentences.original: 欲速则不达。 (Yù sù zé bù dá.)
sentences.translation: Haste makes waste (Desiring speed results in not arriving).

sentences.original: 更上一层。 (Gèng shàng yī céng.)
